skill.name: pytest-recording
skill.description: Guides pytest-recording and VCR.py usage for recording, replaying, sanitizing, and debugging HTTP interactions in Python tests.
skill.summary: Record, replay, sanitize, and debug HTTP cassettes for pytest tests.
skill.author: bossjones

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, network, environment, weak crypto, and heuristic combination findings. Review found the command and weak crypto hits are documentation examples or terminology, not executable skill code; the real risk is that VCR cassettes can record live HTTP data and may expose secrets if filtering is incomplete.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Data May Be Recorded in HTTP Cassettes
medium_findings.description: The skill teaches recording and replaying live HTTP interactions with pytest-recording. It includes filtering guidance, but incomplete filter lists can still allow authorization headers, API keys, cookies, or response secrets into YAML cassettes that may be committed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The guide explicitly covers secret filtering and warns users to verify cassettes do not contain secrets. This confirms a real data exposure risk, although the skill also provides mitigation guidance.

medium_findings.title: Live Network Requests Are Part of Normal Use
medium_findings.description: The guide documents options that disable replay or create new recordings, which can make live requests to configured services. This is expected for pytest-recording, but users should avoid running these modes against production APIs without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented pytest flags intentionally record or refresh HTTP interactions. The behavior is legitimate, but it can contact external services depending on the project tests.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documented Shell Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many backtick and command patterns. The reviewed lines are Markdown code fences, inline option names, or examples such as pytest, cat, and grep; no skill file executes these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown guidance and code examples. There is no wrapper script, hook, or executable source that runs shell commands on behalf of the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment Access and Hardcoded IP
low_findings.description: The env_access hit is a query parameter name used in a VCR filter list, not an environment variable read. The hardcoded IP is localhost in an ignore_hosts example and does not indicate external network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 84 lists key names to redact, and line 90 lists localhost addresses to ignore. Neither line accesses secrets or sends data to a remote host.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography findings appear to be keyword collisions with pytest-recording terminology and record mode names. No cryptographic algorithm selection or hashing code appears in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations describe recording HTTP interactions and refreshing cassettes. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, insecure cipher use, or custom cryptography in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Scanner Heuristic Dismissed After Review
dangerous_patterns.description: The critical code execution plus network plus credential-access heuristic is not confirmed. The skill contains documentation for pytest-recording, local test commands, localhost ignore configuration, and secret filtering guidance; no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration instruction was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combined static signals are present only as documentation examples and safety guidance. The remaining concern is operational misuse of HTTP recording, not malicious skill behavior.

content.value_statement: Live API tests are slow, flaky, and can leak credentials into test artifacts. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create sanitized pytest-recording workflows with reusable HTTP cassettes.
actual_capabilities: Explains pytest-recording record modes such as none, once, all, rewrite, and new_episodes.
Shows how to mark pytest tests with VCR cassette names and multiple cassettes.
Provides vcr_config examples for headers, query parameters, match criteria, and ignored hosts.
Guides response sanitization with pytest_recording_configure and before_record_response.
Offers troubleshooting steps for missing cassettes, request mismatches, stale recordings, and cassette review.
Lists common LLM provider authentication headers that should be filtered from recordings.
limitations: It is a Markdown guide, not an executable pytest plugin or validation tool.
It cannot prove that a project cassette is free of secrets without inspecting that project.
It does not cover every API provider authentication scheme or custom header name.
It assumes the project already uses pytest and can install pytest-recording or VCR.py.

use_cases.title: Add VCR Coverage to API Tests
use_cases.description: Create pytest tests that record HTTP responses once and replay them for deterministic local and CI runs.

use_cases.title: Sanitize LLM Provider Recordings
use_cases.description: Configure header and query parameter filtering before recording requests to OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, Google AI, or Cohere.

use_cases.title: Debug Failing Cassette Replays
use_cases.description: Investigate missing cassettes, stale responses, and request matcher differences when replayed tests fail.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic VCR Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one API test.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add pytest-recording to a Python test that calls an external HTTP API. Include the marker, cassette naming, and a safe default record mode.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Secret Filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my pytest vcr_config and suggest filters for authorization headers, API keys, query tokens, cookies, and sensitive response headers.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Cassette Mismatch
prompt_templates.prompt: A pytest-recording test cannot match an existing cassette. Walk me through comparing match_on criteria, request paths, query strings, and stale interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Recording Policy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team workflow for CI and pull requests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pytest-recording policy for a team that tests multiple LLM providers. Cover record modes, cassette review, regeneration, secret scanning, and CI behavior.

output_examples.input: I need a safe starting configuration for pytest-recording with API keys.
output: A module-scoped VCR configuration that filters authorization, api-key, x-api-key, and token query parameters.
Matcher guidance using method, scheme, host, port, path, and query for stable replay.
A reminder to inspect generated cassettes before committing them.

output_examples.input: My cassette replay fails after an API response changed.
output: A focused checklist for stale cassette review.
Steps to regenerate only the affected cassette before using a broad rewrite.
Advice to compare the recorded request against the current request shape.

best_practices: Define secret filters before the first recording and review every new cassette before commit.
Use restrictive replay modes in CI so tests do not make unexpected live requests.
Keep cassettes small, named clearly, and regenerated only when behavior changes.
anti_patterns: Recording live traffic before filtering authentication headers, cookies, and token query parameters.
Using broad rewrite modes in shared CI jobs without review or service limits.
Committing large cassettes that contain unrelated responses or unstable provider metadata.

faq.question: Does this skill run pytest commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user or agent decides whether to run commands in the project.

faq.question: Can it prevent every secret from entering cassettes?
faq.answer: No. It explains common filters, but each project must review provider-specific headers, query parameters, cookies, and response bodies.

faq.question: Which pytest-recording modes does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers none, once, new_episodes, all, and rewrite, plus disabling recording for live requests.

faq.question: Is it useful for LLM provider tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes common authentication header names for OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, Google AI, and Cohere.

faq.question: Does it include cassette debugging guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers missing cassette files, request mismatch checks, stale recordings, and ways to inspect cassette contents.

faq.question: Should cassettes be reviewed in pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends reviewing cassettes to check for sensitive data leaks and unnecessary recorded content.
